name: Buckwheat

book_claims.claim: Whole grains including pseudocereals associated with protective effects against obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease and cancer
book_claims.mechanism: Bran contains phyto-protectants with antioxidative and anticarcinogenic activities. Provides fibre, vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals processed by gut microbiome into protective metabolites.

supplementary_research.finding: A 2018 systematic review (Food Chem, PMID 29544922) found that buckwheat consumption was associated with reduced blood glucose, total cholesterol, and blood pressure, attributed to its high rutin and D-chiro-inositol content.

A 2006 study (J Agric Food Chem, PMID 16176615) found that buckwheat protein extract significantly lowered cholesterol in hamsters through increased fecal excretion of steroids, with effects comparable to soy protein.

A 2015 review (J Agric Food Chem, PMID 25694675) documented buckwheat as the richest dietary source of D-chiro-inositol, a compound that improves insulin sensitivity and shows promise for type 2 diabetes management.

A 2007 study in a Chinese population (Am J Clin Nutr, PMID 17093136) found that regular buckwheat consumption was associated with lower blood glucose, lower blood pressure, and improved lipid profiles compared to non-consumers.

culinary_pairings.source: Kasha varnishkes and mushroom-buckwheat porridge traditions
Traditional soba with dashi and soy; buckwheat flour is the base
Galettes de sarrasin (Breton crepes); kasha cooked in butter
Classic kasha varnishkes with caramelized onions and bow-tie pasta
Buckwheat honey is a traditional pairing; sweetens bitterness
Galette filling; tangy dairy complements earthy grain

seasonality: Available year-round as dried groats or flour. Toasted buckwheat (kasha) has deeper flavor. Raw groats are milder. Buckwheat flour best stored in fridge or freezer.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Flavonoid glycoside with anti-inflammatory and capillary-strengthening properties. Converted to quercetin by gut bacteria. Not found in any true cereal grain.
Insulin-mimetic compound that improves insulin signaling. Shows promise for type 2 diabetes and PCOS management.
Unlike true cereals, buckwheat protein contains all essential amino acids in good proportions including lysine. Protein quality comparable to animal sources (PDCAAS 0.7).
Excellent source. Important for insulin sensitivity and over 300 enzymatic reactions.
Includes resistant starch and soluble fiber. Supports gut microbiome diversity and SCFA production.

synergies.description: While buckwheat has complete protein, combining with legumes further improves amino acid balance
Vitamin C from vegetables improves iron absorption from buckwheat
